analyse_listing_photos|{\"image_urls\":: {"overall_condition":"good","confidence":"high","summary":"The apartment shows a competent 2022 renovation of kitchen and bathrooms with modern finishes, combined with selective updates to living spaces. The property is well-lit and functionally sound.","observations":["Kitchen features modern appliances (dual ovens, modern hood, induction cooktop visible), bright lime-green tile backsplash, and light wood cabinetry in good condition","Two bathrooms present: main bathroom is large with full shower enclosure, heated towel rails, clean tile work, and mirror with lighting; second bathroom appears compact but functional","Open-plan living/kitchen layout maximizes perceived space and light from multiple windows and balcony doors","Windows are modern double-glazed units with good sealing; natural light is abundant throughout","Flooring transitions show practical choices: tile in wet areas, ceramic in living spaces, and wooden parquet in bedrooms","Multiple bedrooms visible with modern sliding wardrobes (one with black frame/frosted panels, one with yellow and blue storage units)","Entrance hallway and circulation spaces are well-organized with good sight lines","Radiator heating visible throughout; infrastructure appears functional"],"red_flags":["Kitchen appliances (ovens, cooktop) appear modern but seller claims 2022 renovation without appliance replacement—visual evidence contradicts this claim","Bathroom fixtures (sinks, some elements) show mixed ages; some elements appear pre-2022 despite renovation claim","Minor scuff marks and wear visible on white walls and woodwork, inconsistent with 'newly renovated' claim for a property allegedly updated 2 years ago","Hallway walls show slight discoloration/marks suggesting incomplete final touches to 2022 work","One bathroom image shows tile work with potential minor grouting issues and wear","Generic/budget pendant lights throughout suggest fixture replacement was not prioritized in claimed renovation","Storage room visible in one image appears sparse and minimal in fit-out quality","Balcony condition not clearly visible in photos—cannot verify maintenance state"],"photos_analysed":15,"model_used":"claude-haiku-4-5"}

get_property_details|{\"url\": \"https:/: {"url":"https://www.otodom.pl/pl/oferta/mieszkanie-3-pokojowe-z-miejscem-parkingowym-na-osiedlu-borki-ID4Bn1m","id":"68011976","title":"Mieszkanie 3-pokojowe z miejscem parkingowym na osiedlu Borki","price_pln":960000,"price_per_m2_pln":13675,"monthly_community_fee_pln":null,"surface_m2":70.2,"rooms":3,"floor":"floor_1","total_floors":1,"build_year":2002,"building_type":null,"building_material":"brick","construction_status":"ready_to_use","windows_type":"plastic","heating":"gas","ownership_form":"full_ownership","market":"secondary","free_from":"2026-05-18","address":{"street":"ul. Południowa","district":"Wawer","subdistrict":"Nadwiśle","city":"Warszawa","province":"mazowieckie","postal_code":null},"coordinates":{"latitude":52.1880075,"longitude":21.1493825},"amenities":["internet","telewizja kablowa","domofon / wideofon","monitoring / ochrona","teren zamknięty","pralka","zmywarka","lodówka","piekarnik","balkon","pom. użytkowe","garaż/miejsce parkingowe","ogródek"],"description_pl":"Do sprzedania trzypokojowe, słoneczne i ustawne mieszkanie o pow. 70,2 m2 na kameralnym, ogrodzonym i strzeżonym osiedlu Borki w Warszawie (dzielnica Wawer). Mieszkanie usytuowane na pierwszym piętrze jednopiętrowego, cztero-mieszkaniowego budynku z cegły z 2002 r. Mieszkanie stanowi odrębną własność z Księgą Wieczystą. Mieszkanie składa się z salonu o pow. 19,4 m2, z wyjściem na balkon o powierzchni 5,8 m2, połączonego z kuchnią (z oknem) o pow. 9,2 m2, dwóch sypialni o pow. 10,3 m2 i 13,5 m2. W mieszkaniu znajduje się łazienka (z oknem) o pow. 6 m2, pralnia/WC 1,3 m2 oraz przedpokój o powierzchni 8,4 m2 z 2 pojemnymi szafami wnękowymi. Do mieszkania przynależy schowek o powierzchni 2,1 m2 oraz miejsce parkingowe. Istnieje możliwość instalacji kominka. Możliwość podłączenia internetu i telewizji kablowej. Mieszkanie wykończone, gotowe do wprowadzenia (odnowione w 2022 roku, meble oraz sprzęty w kuchni i łazience nie były wymieniane). Obecnie mieszkanie jest niezamieszkane (zakończenie najmu), więc do szybkiego wprowadzenia. Wszystkie media miejskie - gaz, woda, prąd i kanalizacja. Na terenie osiedla są dwa place zabaw i boisko oraz miejsca postojowe dla gości. W najbliższej okolicy znajdują się: żłobek, przedszkole, szkoła, przychodnia i komunikacja miejska. W pobliżu korty tenisowe, basen, ścieżka rowerowa, market spożywczy, stacja paliw oraz tereny zielone przyległe do Wisły. Około 3 km do SKM (Międzylesie lub Radość) oraz nieco ponad 3,5 km do Południowej Obwodnicy Warszawy (na tyle daleko, że nie przeszkadza i na tyle blisko, że można szybko dojechać na Wilanów/Ursynów). Dogodny dojazd do centrum Wałem Miedzeszyńskim. Nie zwlekaj, skontaktuj się i umów na prezentację już dziś! {"query":"Warsaw rental demand drivers oversupply risk outer districts","excerpts":[{"source":"nbp-quarterly-2021-Q1","content":"m the group of ten other cities. The uncertainty surrounding the pandemic, including concerns about the health of the relatives and the need to work and study at the place of residence, resulted in an

increased interest in renting rooms and buying or renting houses. The unprecedented situation could have become an important rationale behind the decision to buy one's own home instead of continuing the rental, which could have added to the increase in the supply of rental housing. The impact of these factors has created a significant oversupply in the residential rental market. Continued oversupply was confirmed in February 2021 by the majority of respondents (73%). A significant decline in student demand affected as many as 90% of respondents. In September 2020, students entered into tenancy agreements for the 2020/2021 academic year through real estate agents , but the decision concerning on-line studies could have urged them to terminate contracts at short notice. According to the respondents, the decline in demand for domestic rentals in February 2021 reached an average of 21% in year-on-year terms. The deepest decline was recorded in the group of six cities (by 26%). The decrease in the demand generated by foreigners from Eastern Europe was estimated at 19%. Growing supply of housing for rent recorded since 2020 Q2 was confirmed by NBP data and monitoring of the number of offers on the websites. The number of dwelling rental offers in Warsaw rose by 84% y/y in 2020 Q3 and by 83% in 2020 Q4, compared to the corresponding periods in the previous year, in the group of six cities by - 91% and 78%, respectively and in the group of ten cities by 30% and 73%, respectively. The ratio of the number of home rental transactions concluded through real estate agents in a given quarter to the number of off ers held at the beginning of the quarter is an indicator of the condition of the rental market. According to a simplified approach, this relationship indicates the percentage of dwellings that have found tenants. In 2020 Q4, in Warsaw, real estate agents rented 31% of dwellings on offer compared to 52% in 2019 Q4, which, amid growing offer, confirms that the demand is","similarity":0.5845},{"source":"nbp-quarterly-2021-Q1","content":"trictions imposed in 2020 due to the COVID -19 pandemic triggered a rapid and significant downturn in the residential rental market, resulting in a sharp increase in vacancy rates, a decline in rental

Information on home prices and the situation in the housing and commercial real estate market in Poland in 2021 Q1 Narodowy Bank Polski 39 income and increased competition among landlords and a downward adj ustment of rent rates. University studies continued via on-line learning made it possible for students renting rooms or dwellings to give up their tenancy, which has “released” the supply of thousands of dwellings in university cities. The large-scale of on- line office work allowed workers renting dwellings to return to their permanent residence. The first months of the pandemic also saw the return to the home country of individuals from abroad, including those providing labour. Most of these people, mainly from across the eastern border, returned to work in Poland at the beginning of 2020 Q3, consequently reducing the resulting oversupply. According to agents, this group was the most important component generating demand for rental housing in 2020 after the spread of the pandemic. Employment growth, which had driven the rental market in earlier years, gave way to small declines recorded in most cities in 2020, additionally limiting the growth potential of “new demand” for home rental. The extent of job losses recorded in some cities, mainly in retail and services sectors, may have translated into increased vacancy rates. An additional important element of the increase in oversupply of dwellings for rent was the supply of more dwellings purchased on the prima ry market for investment purposes, scheduled to enter the rental market in 2020. An increase in the number of rental offers of new dwellings (built after 2014) during the pandemic period was noted by 67% of respondents from six large cities, more than half of respondents from Warsaw and the least (41%) from the group of ten other cities.
